The length of this war tested David’s faith and patience, and made his eventual settlement all the more welcome. The conflict between grace and corruption in the hearts of believers can be compared to this warfare. There is a long war between them, with the flesh lusting against the spirit, and the spirit against the flesh; but as the work of holiness continues, corruption grows weaker and weaker, like the house of Saul, while grace grows stronger and stronger, like the house of David.

Many, like Abner, are not above committing shameful acts, who are too proud to accept correction or even the suspicion of being guilty. While people continue in sin, and seemingly without concern, they are often conscious that they are fighting against God. Many intend to serve their own purposes, and will betray those who trust them when they can gain any advantage. Yet the Lord serves his own designs, even by those who are thus motivated by revenge, ambition, or lust; but because they do not intend to honor him, in the end they will be cast aside with contempt. There was real generosity both to Michal and to the memory of Saul, in David’s receiving her, remembering probably how once he owed his life to her affection, and knowing that she was separated from him partly by her father's authority. Let no person set their heart on what they are not entitled to. If any disagreement has separated husband and wife, as they expect God’s blessing, let them be reconciled and live together in love.

Judgments are prepared for scorners like Abner; but Joab acted wickedly in what he did. David deeply grieved over Abner's murder, and expressed his detestation of it in many ways. The guilt of bloodshed brings a curse upon families: if people do not avenge it, God will. It is sad to die like a fool, as those who shorten their own days do, and those who make no provision for another world. Who would desire power when a person may have the name of it, must be accountable for it, yet is restricted in its use? David should have done his duty and then trusted God with the outcome. Secular strategy spared Joab. The Son of David may delay long, but never fails to punish unrepentant sinners. He who now reigns upon the throne of David has a kingdom of a nobler kind. Whatever He does is noticed by all his willing people, and is pleasing to them.
